training_loop.name: Failure capture
training_loop.description: When a deterministic rule fires but the user marks the result as wrong, or when the user submits a prompt that returns low confidence, capture the failure event and store it with the input DAX, the rule that fired (if any), the user-provided correct answer, and the current page context.

training_loop.name: Screenshot and context snapshot
training_loop.description: On every Act tab action that involves vision or action planning, store: screenshot base64, page URL, page mode, detected fields, timestamp, and the action plan output. Store in chrome.storage.local with a 30-item circular buffer.

training_loop.name: Claude rule proposal
training_loop.description: When the fallback (Claude) solves a problem with confidence > 0.85 and the issue appears generalizable, Claude returns a suggested_rule in the fallback contract. This rule is queued in chrome.storage.local as a pending_rule candidate.

training_loop.name: Rule compilation
training_loop.description: A Node.js compile script reads rules_advanced.json, correctness_rules.json, performance_rules.json, modeling_rules.json, and pending_rules.json. It deduplicates by pattern string similarity and function set overlap. It assigns IDs, priority scores, and confidence values. Output is written to rules/processed/final_rules.json and compiled_rules.json.

training_loop.name: Test generation
training_loop.description: For each new rule promoted to final_rules.json, generate at least one positive test case (DAX that should trigger it), one negative test case (valid DAX that looks similar), and one edge case (false-positive trap). Store in training/test_suite.json.

training_loop.name: False positive prevention
training_loop.description: Run the full test suite against the compiled rule engine. Any rule that fires on a negative test case is flagged. Any rule that fires on a false-positive edge case has its confidence reduced by 0.05. Rules with confidence below 0.70 after adjustment are moved to requires_llm: true and removed from deterministic execution.

training_loop.name: Coverage measurement
training_loop.description: After each compile cycle, report: total rules by category, rules with at least one positive test, rules with at least one negative test, rules with at least one edge test, rules in needs_review vs safe, rules in requires_llm, average confidence by category.

training_loop.name: final_rules.json update
training_loop.description: When a rule passes all tests and meets promotion criteria, it is merged into final_rules.json with a promoted_at timestamp. The pending_rules.json entry is removed. The compile script is re-run to update compiled_rules.json.

training_loop.name: Fallback criteria review
training_loop.description: Monthly review of all rules that are currently requires_llm: true. If a pattern has appeared 10+ times in the failure log with consistent detection logic, promote it to deterministic with appropriate confidence. If a rule has been requires_llm for 6+ months with no promotion path, archive it.

coverage_metrics: total_rules: total count of rules in final_rules.json
rules_with_positive_test: count of rules with at least one positive_case in test_suite.json
rules_with_negative_test: count of rules with at least one negative_case
rules_with_edge_test: count of rules with at least one edge_case
safe_fix_pct: count of safe rules / total_rules * 100
needs_review_pct: count of needs_review rules / total_rules * 100
requires_llm_pct: count of requires_llm rules / total_rules * 100
average_confidence_correctness: mean confidence for correctness rules
average_confidence_performance: mean confidence for performance rules
average_confidence_modeling: mean confidence for modeling rules
pending_rule_count: count of rules in pending_rules.json awaiting promotion
promotion_criteria: Rule has at least 1 positive test case that passes
Rule has at least 1 negative test case that does NOT fire
Rule has at least 1 edge case defined
Rule does not overlap with an existing rule by more than 2 shared functions AND 2 shared conditions
Rule fix_type is correct: safe only if the fix provably preserves business logic
Rule has been validated against real-world DAX from at least 2 different domains
fallback_criteria: Input contains screenshot or visual description that requires vision analysis
Input describes a multi-step browser automation goal that requires planning
Input involves RLS or security changes — always escalate
Input involves destructive operations: delete, publish, overwrite, export
Input involves XMLA, REST API, or direct model write operations
Rule fires but fix_type is needs_review and user has not approved
Detection logic conditions are partially met but not all — confidence below threshold
